question: In the NuScale design, is AC or DC power required for safety shutdown and cooling?

nuscale_ch01_0252.title: DSRS 8.3.1, Rev 0: AC Power Systems (Onsite)II.5 Compliance with GDC 18
nuscale_ch01_0252.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with various General Design Criteria (GDC) and regulatory requirements for onsite AC power systems. While NuScale conforms to some GDCs (such as GDC 2, 4, and 50), it partially conforms or departs from others (such as GDC 5, 17, 18, 33, and 34-44), often supporting exemptions or using alternative principal design criteria. Specific details and justifications for these positions are referenced in other sections of the safety analysis report.

nuscale_ch01_0267.title: DSRS 8.3.2, Rev 0: DC Power Systems (Onsite)II.1 Conformance with RG 1.32
nuscale_ch01_0267.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for onsite DC power systems. It notes that while the design conforms to several General Design Criteria (GDC), it departs from others (GDC 17, 18, 33, 34, 35, 38, 41, and 44) by supporting exemptions or alternative criteria. Additionally, conformance with Regulatory Guide 1.32 is only partial, applying to specific aspects of the Emergency DC Supply System (EDSS) design.

nuscale_ch01_0269.title: DSRS 8.3.2, Rev 0: DC Power Systems (Onsite)II.3 Conformance with RG 1.81
nuscale_ch01_0269.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for onsite DC power systems. It details compliance or departures from specific General Design Criteria (GDC) and regulatory guides, noting that while NuScale conforms to some requirements, it seeks exemptions or alternative criteria for others, particularly GDC 17, 18, 33, and several others, and partially conforms to RG 1.32 for certain design aspects.

nuscale_ch01_0325.title: SRP 9.2.2, Rev 4: Reactor Auxiliary Cooling Water SystemII.5 Cooling Water System Inspection
nuscale_ch01_0325.summary: The section states that the NuScale Reactor Auxiliary Cooling Water System does not serve a safety-related cooling or accident mitigation function, so the inspection requirements of SRP 9.2.2, Rev 4, II.5 are not applicable. This is consistent with previous acceptance criteria, as the system is not classified as safety-related.

nuscale_ch01_0391.title: SRP 9.5.1.2, Rev 0: Risk-Informed, Performance-Based Fire Protection ProgramAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0391.summary: The section explains that the development and implementation of a risk-informed, performance-based fire protection program (SRP 9.5.1.2, Rev 0) is not applicable to the NuScale design certification application. Instead, this responsibility lies with Combined License (COL) applicants who reference the NuScale design and choose to implement the relevant regulatory provisions.

nuscale_ch01_0324.title: SRP 9.2.2, Rev 4: Reactor Auxiliary Cooling Water SystemII.4 Cooling Water System
nuscale_ch01_0324.summary: This section explains that the NuScale Reactor Auxiliary Cooling Water System does not serve a safety-related cooling or accident mitigation function, so many regulatory criteria are not applicable. For criteria that are partially applicable, the system conforms to requirements for nonsafety-related portions, such as being designed as Seismic Category II to prevent impacts on safety-related structures, systems, and components.

nuscale_ch01_0251.title: DSRS 8.3.1, Rev 0: AC Power Systems (Onsite)II.4 Compliance with GDC 17
nuscale_ch01_0251.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with various General Design Criteria (GDC) for onsite AC power systems. While the design conforms to some GDCs (such as GDC 2, 4, and 50), it departs from others (including GDC 17, 18, 33, and 34-44), for which NuScale seeks exemptions or uses alternative principal design criteria. The document references specific sections for detailed explanations of these compliance positions.

nuscale_ch01_0387.title: SRP 9.5.1.1, Rev 0: Fire Protection ProgramII.4 Fire Protection for Permanently Shutdown and Decommissioning Reactor Plants
nuscale_ch01_0387.summary: This section explains that the fire protection requirements for permanently shutdown and decommissioning reactor plants (SRP 9.5.1.1, II.4) are not applicable to the NuScale design, as these criteria only apply to licensees who have submitted certifications for license termination under 10 CFR 50.82. The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report clarifies that such provisions are outside the scope of their current licensing basis.

nuscale_ch01_0277.title: DSRS 8.4, Rev 0: Station BlackoutII.3 Independence of SBO-related power sources
nuscale_ch01_0277.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding the independence of Station Blackout (SBO)-related power sources. It notes that while the design partially conforms to certain regulatory guides, the NuScale plant can perform all safety-related functions for 72 hours without AC power, and the independence of SBO-related power supplies is addressed in referenced sections. Some requirements, such as alternate AC power sources, are deemed not applicable due to the plant’s passive safety design.

nuscale_ch01_0022.title: Engineered Safety Feature Materials
nuscale_ch01_0022.summary: The section describes how the NuScale reactor’s containment vessel (CNV) is maintained under vacuum to reduce corrosion, facilitate leak detection, minimize heat transfer, and limit oxygen for safety. In the event of an emergency, passive cooling rapidly reduces pressure and temperature, and the reactor’s design—with passive safety systems and a small fuel inventory—improves safety by eliminating large-break loss-of-coolant accidents and reducing core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0427.title: DSRS 10.3, Rev 0: Main Steam Supply SystemII.4 MSS is capable of supporting core cooling or safe-shutdown (non-DBA) in the event of an SBO (10 CFR 50.63)
nuscale_ch01_0427.summary: The section reviews the NuScale Main Steam Supply System (MSS) against regulatory criteria, focusing on its ability to support core cooling or safe shutdown during a station blackout (SBO) as required by 10 CFR 50.63. It concludes that the MSS partially conforms to this requirement, as the NuScale plant’s passive design and reduced reliance on AC power generally meet the intent of the guidance, though not all specific criteria are fully addressed.

nuscale_ch01_0278.title: SRP Appendix 8-A, Rev1: General Agenda, Station Site VisitsAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0278.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to DC power systems and station blackout requirements. It notes that NuScale partially conforms to several regulatory guides, with some requirements not applicable due to the design’s passive safety features and the responsibilities assigned to the COL applicant. Additionally, SRP Appendix 8-A is identified as not applicable, as it pertains to staff site visits during licensing reviews rather than design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0028.title: Instrumentation, Controls, and Electrical Systems
nuscale_ch01_0028.summary: The Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) in the NuScale design provides passive decay heat removal during a loss-of-coolant accident using independent vent and recirculation valves, operating without active power. The Control Room Habitability System (CRHS) passively supplies clean, compressed air to protect operators from hazardous releases, maintaining positive pressure for at least 72 hours. Fission product removal relies on the containment vessel (CNV) and isolation system, with passive control inherent to the module’s design.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) scenarios and reduce reliance on external power, resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

edges.description: Section A addresses the regulatory compliance and applicability of onsite AC power systems for safety functions, while Section B addresses the regulatory compliance and applicability of onsite DC power systems, with both sections providing information necessary to determine whether AC or DC power is required for safety shutdown and cooling in the NuScale design.

Section A discusses the regulatory compliance and applicability of onsite AC power systems for safety functions, while Section B addresses the regulatory compliance of onsite DC power systems, together providing information on whether either AC or DC power is required for safety shutdown and cooling in the NuScale design.

Section A discusses regulatory compliance for onsite and offsite AC power systems, while Section B clarifies that the site cooling water system is not safety-related and does not require power for safety shutdown or accident mitigation, thus Section B supports the implication in Section A that neither AC nor DC power is required for safety shutdown and cooling in the NuScale design.

Section A discusses compliance with AC power system requirements and regulatory criteria for the NuScale design, while Section B addresses fire protection program requirements that COL applicants must implement, with NuScale acknowledging applicability to the design certification; both sections are connected through their reference to regulatory conformance for safety systems but address different aspects (power vs. fire protection) relevant to overall plant safety.

Section A discusses the regulatory compliance and requirements for onsite AC power systems in the NuScale design, while Section B clarifies that the site cooling water system is not safety-related and does not require power for accident mitigation, indicating that neither AC nor DC power is required for safety shutdown and cooling in this context.

Section A and Section B both address compliance with regulatory criteria for onsite AC power systems in the NuScale design, with Section A focusing on GDC 18 and Section B on GDC 17, and both referencing the same FSAR and DSRS sections to demonstrate conformance.

Section A discusses compliance with AC power system requirements for safety shutdown and cooling, while Section B clarifies that the NuScale design does not rely on engineered safety feature (ESF) ventilation systems (which could require AC or DC power) for accident mitigation, connecting them through the information that NuScale's safety shutdown and cooling do not depend on ESF ventilation power sources.

Section A and Section B both state that no onsite AC power system equipment is required for compliance, implying that DC power systems are the focus for meeting regulatory criteria for safety shutdown and cooling in the NuScale design.

Section A states that no onsite AC power system equipment is required for safety functions, while Section B clarifies that the site cooling water system does not serve a safety-related function, together indicating that neither AC power nor the associated cooling water systems are required for safety shutdown and cooling in the NuScale design.

Section A discusses the regulatory compliance and requirements for onsite AC and DC power systems in the NuScale design, while Section B addresses the fire protection program requirements, which reference the need for reliable power (AC or DC) to support safety functions such as shutdown and cooling, thus connecting the two sections through the dependency of fire protection and safety functions on the power systems described in Section A.

Section A states that no onsite AC power system equipment is required for safety shutdown and cooling, while Section B indicates that the site cooling water system does not serve a safety-related cooling or accident mitigation function, together implying that neither AC power nor the conventional cooling water system is required for safety functions in the NuScale design.

Section A states that no onsite AC power system equipment is required for safety shutdown and cooling, while Section B discusses compliance with AC power system requirements, indicating that AC power is not required for these safety functions in the NuScale design.

Section A discusses the regulatory compliance and requirements for AC and DC power systems in the NuScale design, while Section B clarifies that the NuScale design does not rely on engineered safety feature (ESF) ventilation systems for accident mitigation, implying that safety shutdown and cooling functions are independent of such systems and thus must rely on the power systems described in Section A.

Section A specifies that no onsite AC power system equipment is required for safety functions, while Section B states that the site cooling water system does not serve a safety-related function, together indicating that neither AC power nor the associated cooling water systems are required for safety shutdown and cooling in the NuScale design.

Section A and Section B both state that the site cooling water system does not serve a safety-related cooling or accident mitigation function, indicating that neither AC nor DC power is required for these functions in the NuScale design.

Section A states that the site cooling water system does not serve a safety-related cooling or accident mitigation function, while Section B clarifies that the NuScale design does not rely on engineered safety feature ventilation systems for accident mitigation, indicating both sections describe systems that are not required for safety-related shutdown or cooling.

Section A and Section B both reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report's conformance with regulatory guidance for DC power systems (DSRS 8.3.2), specifically regarding partial conformance with RG 1.153 and RG 1.63 for the EDSS design, indicating they share the same regulatory evaluation details.
